phases.name: Countered-peer-claims count surfaced on the landing peer line + /peer-claims header (thick walking skeleton + close confirmations)

steps.name: Wire count_countered_peer_claims + the 5th LandingSummary field countered_peer_claims + the REUSED render_countered + render_landing peer-line render + store-threading and show "4 peer claims (1 countered)" on GET / (THICK walking skeleton)
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (PC-WS, US-PC-001 Theme 1 — the riskiest-assumption thread, the deferred PEER sibling of slice-18). It forces the WHOLE countered-PEER-count thread into existence end-to-end: viewer → the LOCAL countered-peer-count aggregate → the extended LandingSummary (5th field) → the SHARED (REUSED) pure render → the front-door summary showing the disputed-peer-claim awareness count. (ports) Add `count_countered_peer_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>` to StoreReadPort — a read-only sibling of the slice-18 `count_countered_own_claims` / the slice-17 `count_peer_claims` / `count_claims` (ADR-056 D1); NO mutation method added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1). (adapter-duckdb) Impl it as ONE aggregate over the SAME shared connection — the EXACT slice-18 SQL with the OUTER table swapped `claims c → peer_claims p`: `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.cid) FROM peer_claims p WHERE p.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` — a PRESENCE count (the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) collapses a peer claim countered by N counterers to ONE membership, no JOIN-fanout, ADR-056 D1), peer-only by query shape (outer table is `peer_claims`), parameter-free / injection-safe (literal `'counters'` only), invariant to store size (both ref columns indexed); the inner UNION IN-set is BYTE-IDENTICAL to slice-18's — only the outer table differs. A cached peer claim is countered by the OPERATOR (her counter in `claim_references`) OR by ANOTHER peer (their counter in `peer_claim_references`, slice-11) — both arms contribute. Names `peer_claims`/`claim_references`/`peer_claim_references` so the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (R-PC-9: `(peer=T, own=F)` → `None`, ADR-056 Enforcement). (viewer-domain) Add a FIFTH `pub countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` field to `LandingSummary` (ADR-056 D2 — additive, parallel to the slice-17 three + the slice-18 fourth, identical degrade semantics) and REUSE the SHARED slice-18 `render_countered(Option<usize>) -> String` helper (ADR-056 D3 / WD-PC-10 — NO new helper): `Some(n) → "(n countered)"`, `Some(0) → "(0 countered)"` (honest zero), `None → "(— countered)"` (missing marker), the copy NEUTRAL disputed-claim awareness (never a verdict/penalty/"disputed by N", C-6). Extend `render_landing(&LandingSummary)` to render `render_countered(summary.countered_peer_claims)` BESIDE the UNCHANGED peer-claims line (the peer-claims `render_count` "4" untouched — additive, never a re-weight, C-4) → "4 peer claims (1 countered)", and the slice-18 own line untouched beside it (WD-PC-7). The pure render stays a TOTAL function of LandingSummary (now 2⁵ Option combinations; every one renders, no panic, no I/O). (adapter-http-viewer) Thread `store.count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` (Result → Option, the per-count independent degrade) into `landing_page`'s LandingSummary build (the 5th `.ok()` resolution), call `render_landing(&summary)`, always 200. (xtask) UNCHANGED at this step — a read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge, `check_viewer_capability_boundary` + `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` stay GREEN. Outcome: a seeded store (4 peer claims, 1 countered by a distinct peer OR the operator) + GET / returns a 200 page showing "4 peer claims (1 countered)" — the peer-claims "4" unchanged beside the genuine seeded presence count, the slice-18 own line untouched. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN (mirrors slice-06..18): the AT spawns the openlore (viewer) bin via ViewerServer::start, so the run MUST cargo build the bin before executing these ATs. NO second binary (the count is a LOCAL DuckDB COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate). The seed (seed_landing_store_with_countered_peer_claims, with the direct ADR-056 read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle) + asserts (assert_landing_peer_countered_count, assert_landing_shows_count) already compile in support/mod.rs (they drive the EXISTING peer add/peer pull/claim counter verbs + scan strings).
criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `peer add` + `peer pull` + counter paths, Pillar 3) with 4 cached peer claims, 1 of them countered (by a distinct peer OR the operator) returns a 200 page showing the peer-claims count "4" UNCHANGED and the countered count "(1 countered)" beside it (assert_landing_peer_countered_count).
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_countered_peer_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.cid) FROM peer_claims p WHERE p.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` over the shared connection — the EXACT slice-18 SQL with the outer table swapped `claims c → peer_claims p`, a presence count (de-duped IN-set + DISTINCT, no JOIN-fanout), peer-only by query shape, parameter-free; the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (R-PC-9, ADR-056 Enforcement).

viewer-domain adds the FIFTH `countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` field to LandingSummary (additive, parallel degrade, ADR-056 D2) and REUSES the SHARED slice-18 `render_countered(Option<usize>) -> String` helper (NO new helper, WD-PC-10); render_landing renders it BESIDE the UNCHANGED peer-claims line (additive, never a re-weight, C-4) with the slice-18 own line untouched (WD-PC-7), as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store.count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` (the 5th independent `.ok()` resolution) into landing_page's LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `peer add` + `peer pull` + counter paths, Pillar 3) with 4 cached peer claims, 1 of them countered (by a distinct peer OR the operator) returns a 200 page showing the peer-claims count "4" UNCHANGED and the countered count "(1 countered)" beside it (assert_landing_peer_countered_count).
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_countered_peer_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.cid) FROM peer_claims p WHERE p.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` over the shared connection — the EXACT slice-18 SQL with the outer table swapped `claims c → peer_claims p`, a presence count (de-duped IN-set + DISTINCT, no JOIN-fanout), peer-only by query shape, parameter-free; the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (R-PC-9, ADR-056 Enforcement).

viewer-domain adds the FIFTH `countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` field to LandingSummary (additive, parallel degrade, ADR-056 D2) and REUSES the SHARED slice-18 `render_countered(Option<usize>) -> String` helper (NO new helper, WD-PC-10); render_landing renders it BESIDE the UNCHANGED peer-claims line (additive, never a re-weight, C-4) with the slice-18 own line untouched (WD-PC-7), as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store.count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` (the 5th independent `.ok()` resolution) into landing_page's LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge).
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (the 5th LandingSummary field countered_peer_claims + the REUSED render_countered render in render_landing beside the unchanged peer-claims line, the slice-18 own line untouched) and the count-only read seam (count_countered_peer_claims). Extend nightly mutation testing to the COUNT(DISTINCT) presence aggregate (the de-duped UNION IN-set so a peer claim countered by N counterers counts once; the outer table is `peer_claims` not `claims`), the render_countered branch over the 5th field (Some(n) renders the number, None renders the marker), and the additive render (the peer-claims "4" is never re-weighted by the countered count; the slice-18 own line is byte-untouched).

steps.name: The /peer-claims header shows the SAME countered count via render_countered (single source), a peer claim countered by two counterers counts once (presence), and either ref table contributes exactly once
steps.description: Close the single-source + presence-once + either-arm contracts (PC-HEADER, US-PC-002 Theme 1 Ex 2 / WD-PC-8 + PC-PRESENCE, US-PC-000/001 Theme 2 / C-4 / BR-PC-1 CARDINAL + PC-EITHER-TABLE, US-PC-000/001 Theme 2 Ex 2 / C-4). (PC-HEADER) Extend the `/peer-claims` list header to render the SAME countered count: `render_peer_claims_page` gains a `countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` parameter (the SAME Option the landing uses, ADR-056 D3), and renders `render_countered(countered_peer_claims)` near the "Peer Claims" h1 / read-only notice; `peer_claims_page` (adapter-http-viewer) resolves `store.count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` and passes it in. Both surfaces resolve from the SAME read method + render through the SAME helper (single source — not a cached value), so the landing "(N countered)" and the `/peer-claims` header "(N countered)" cannot diverge; pinned EQUAL by assert_landing_and_peer_claims_countered_consistent. The `render_peer_claims_page` signature change ripples to its callers/tests (accepted, ADR-056 consequence). (PC-PRESENCE) Over a store where ONE cached peer claim is countered by TWO distinct counterers (one in each ref table — the operator + another peer) and it is the only countered peer claim, GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the COUNT(DISTINCT) over the de-duped UNION IN-set collapses the two distinct-table counters of the SAME peer CID to ONE membership (C-4 / BR-PC-1), and assert_countered_copy_is_neutral confirms no "disputed by N" sum. (PC-EITHER-TABLE) Over a store with one peer claim countered via the `claim_references` arm (operator's own counter) and a SECOND peer claim countered via the `peer_claim_references` arm (another peer's counter), GET / shows "(2 countered)" — each countered peer claim contributes EXACTLY ONCE regardless of which ref table holds its counter (the de-duped UNION across both arms). Confirmatory of the 01-01 aggregate + REUSED helper once they exist; pins the single-source consistency + the presence-once de-dup + the both-arms-contribute-once semantics end-to-end.
criteria: GET /peer-claims over the SAME store (4 peer, 1 countered) renders "(1 countered)" in the list header via render_countered — render_peer_claims_page gains a `countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` param, peer_claims_page resolves `count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` and passes it in (ADR-056 D3).

The landing "(N countered)" == the /peer-claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (single source — the read method + render helper, not a cached value; assert_landing_and_peer_claims_countered_consistent, WD-PC-8).

Over a store where one cached peer claim is countered by TWO distinct counterers (one in each ref table, the only countered peer claim), GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) presence count (C-4 / BR-PC-1 CARDINAL); the copy is neutral (no "disputed by N" sum, assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).

Over a store with one peer claim countered via the `claim_references` arm and a second via the `peer_claim_references` arm (no other countered), GET / shows "(2 countered)" — each countered peer claim contributes EXACTLY ONCE regardless of which ref table holds its counter (the de-duped UNION across both arms, C-4).
acceptance_criteria: GET /peer-claims over the SAME store (4 peer, 1 countered) renders "(1 countered)" in the list header via render_countered — render_peer_claims_page gains a `countered_peer_claims: Option<usize>` param, peer_claims_page resolves `count_countered_peer_claims().ok()` and passes it in (ADR-056 D3).

The landing "(N countered)" == the /peer-claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (single source — the read method + render helper, not a cached value; assert_landing_and_peer_claims_countered_consistent, WD-PC-8).

Over a store where one cached peer claim is countered by TWO distinct counterers (one in each ref table, the only countered peer claim), GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) presence count (C-4 / BR-PC-1 CARDINAL); the copy is neutral (no "disputed by N" sum, assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).

Over a store with one peer claim countered via the `claim_references` arm and a second via the `peer_claim_references` arm (no other countered), GET / shows "(2 countered)" — each countered peer claim contributes EXACTLY ONCE regardless of which ref table holds its counter (the de-duped UNION across both arms, C-4).
steps.mutation_note: The single-source render (BOTH surfaces through render_countered, the /peer-claims header param threaded the SAME `.ok()` value), the presence-once de-dup (a peer claim countered by 2 counterers counts ONCE), and the both-arms-contribute-once UNION (each ref table contributes exactly once) are load-bearing; mutation-test the render_peer_claims_page header render + the COUNT(DISTINCT)/UNION so a mutant that diverges the two surfaces' copy, drops the DISTINCT (rendering "(2 countered)"), drops one UNION arm (mis-counting either-table), or swaps UNION for UNION ALL in a counting context is killed.

steps.name: An honest zero "(0 countered)" on the landing AND in the /peer-claims header (Some(0), distinct from the missing marker; list renders as slice-06/07)
steps.description: The honest-zero edge on both surfaces (PC-ZERO-LANDING, US-PC-001 Theme 3 / C-5 + PC-ZERO-HEADER, US-PC-002 Theme 3 / C-5 — the success side of `0 ≠ missing`). Over a store with 4 cached peer claims, NONE countered (seed_landing_store_no_peer_claim_countered → the count read returns Ok(0) → Some(0)): (PC-ZERO-LANDING) GET / shows "4 peer claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero, DISTINCT from the missing-number marker "(— countered)" and not an omitted count. (PC-ZERO-HEADER) GET /peer-claims shows "(0 countered)" in the header AND renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06/07 (nothing is countered → the slice-13 presence read is empty). Confirmatory once 01-01/01-02 exist: the Some(0) → "(0 countered)" render branch is exercised on both surfaces, and the assertion that "(— countered)" is ABSENT pins the success side of the type-level `0 ≠ missing` distinction (WD-PC-6; the failure side is pinned by 02-01 / 03-01).
criteria: GET / over a store with 4 cached peer claims and NONE countered (seed_landing_store_no_peer_claim_countered → the count read returns Some(0)) shows "4 peer claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero (assert_landing_peer_countered_count(body, 0)).
The missing marker "(— countered)" is ABSENT on the landing — an honest empty-counter peer store is a successful read of 0, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, C-5 / WD-PC-6).
GET /peer-claims over the same none-countered store shows "(0 countered)" in the header (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_count(body, 0)).

The /peer-claims list renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06/07 (the slice-13 presence read is empty when nothing is countered) — the header "(0 countered)" is the only countered text.
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a store with 4 cached peer claims and NONE countered (seed_landing_store_no_peer_claim_countered → the count read returns Some(0)) shows "4 peer claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero (assert_landing_peer_countered_count(body, 0)).
The missing marker "(— countered)" is ABSENT on the landing — an honest empty-counter peer store is a successful read of 0, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, C-5 / WD-PC-6).
GET /peer-claims over the same none-countered store shows "(0 countered)" in the header (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_count(body, 0)).

The /peer-claims list renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06/07 (the slice-13 presence read is empty when nothing is countered) — the header "(0 countered)" is the only countered text.
steps.mutation_note: The Some(0) → "(0 countered)" (not the missing marker) render branch on both surfaces is the success side of `0 ≠ missing`; mutation-test render_countered so a mutant that renders Some(0) as "(— countered)" (or blanks the zero), or one that leaks a per-row Countered flag when the slice-13 presence set is empty, is killed.

phases.name: The edges — missing≠zero degrade (new cfg-gated 4th-distinct fault seam + xtask guard token), additive no-regression, own-untouched, read-only, offline, no-N+1, anti-misread

steps.name: A failed countered-peer-count read degrades to "(— countered)" on BOTH surfaces without a 5xx (the genuinely-new cfg-gated 4th-DISTINCT fault seam OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT + xtask release-gate guard token)
steps.description: The one step carrying genuinely-NEW fault-injection work (PC-DEGRADE-LANDING + PC-DEGRADE-HEADER, US-PC-000/001/002 Theme 4 / C-2 / C-5 CARDINAL — the per-count independent missing≠zero degrade). Maria's countered-peer-count read fails mid-request while the peer-claims count + the slice-18 own counts + the list read still succeed. (PC-DEGRADE-LANDING) GET / returns a NORMAL 200, the peer-claims count + the slice-18 own line ("12 own claims (3 countered)") + the nav hub still render, and the countered-peer count renders the missing marker "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"), no raw stack trace. (PC-DEGRADE-HEADER) GET /peer-claims returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)", and the list rows + their slice-13 per-row flags still render (the countered row CID present) — the countered-peer-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-056 D4). The per-count independent degrade: each count is resolved by its OWN `.ok()` (Result → Option), so a failed `count_countered_peer_claims` maps to None → render_countered(None) → marker while the siblings (incl. the slice-18 own count) stay Some(_) → number (ADR-056 D2/D4). DELIVER materializes the test-only effect-shell fault seam `start_viewer_with_failing_countered_peer_count` → the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` env var — the 4th DISTINCT token so the PEER count fails INDEPENDENTLY of the slice-18 own count, MIRRORING slice-18's `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT`: a `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated branch substituting `Err(StoreReadError)` for the real `count_countered_peer_claims()` on BOTH the `/` and `/peer-claims` handlers so the SAME production `.ok()` degrade branch runs, plus the `#[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]` release identity (NO env-var read compiled in). The DISTILL harness already references `start_viewer_with_failing_countered_peer_count` + the `start_inner` extra param (a todo!() RED scaffold until DELIVER materializes it). (xtask) Add the NEW release-gate guard token: append the 4th token `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` to the VIEWER_FAIL_SEAM_TOKENS seam-guard scan (check_arch.rs, mirroring the slice-16/17/18 tokens) so an UNGATED read in a release build fails check-arch — the slice-16/17/18 tokens stay, the slice-19 one is added. This pins Earned Trust on the store-read dependency: the design exercises the substrate LYING (a transient StoreReadError) and proves BOTH orientation surfaces survive it without 5xx and without fabricating 0, INDEPENDENTLY of the slice-18 own count.
criteria: GET / against a viewer whose countered-peer-count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_countered_peer_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT; peer + slice-18 own + other reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6); the peer-claims count + the slice-18 own line ("12 own claims (3 countered)", assert_landing_own_line_untouched) + the nav hub still render, and the countered-peer count renders "(— countered)" (assert_landing_peer_countered_missing, NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"); no raw stack trace leaks.

GET /peer-claims against the same failing viewer returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)" (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_missing), and the list rows + their slice-13 per-row flags still render (the seeded countered row CID present) — the countered-peer-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-056 D4).

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` on BOTH the `/` and `/peer-claims` handlers (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-18's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT, a 4th DISTINCT token failing independently of the own count), threaded via the `start_inner` extra param; the per-count `.ok()` degrade (None → render_countered(None) → marker) is the production path under test.

The xtask release-gate seam-guard scan (VIEWER_FAIL_SEAM_TOKENS) is extended to ALSO cover the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the slice-16/17/18 tokens stay and the slice-19 4th token is appended.
acceptance_criteria: GET / against a viewer whose countered-peer-count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_countered_peer_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT; peer + slice-18 own + other reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6); the peer-claims count + the slice-18 own line ("12 own claims (3 countered)", assert_landing_own_line_untouched) + the nav hub still render, and the countered-peer count renders "(— countered)" (assert_landing_peer_countered_missing, NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"); no raw stack trace leaks.

GET /peer-claims against the same failing viewer returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)" (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_missing), and the list rows + their slice-13 per-row flags still render (the seeded countered row CID present) — the countered-peer-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-056 D4).

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` on BOTH the `/` and `/peer-claims` handlers (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-18's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT, a 4th DISTINCT token failing independently of the own count), threaded via the `start_inner` extra param; the per-count `.ok()` degrade (None → render_countered(None) → marker) is the production path under test.

The xtask release-gate seam-guard scan (VIEWER_FAIL_SEAM_TOKENS) is extended to ALSO cover the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_PEER_COUNT` token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the slice-16/17/18 tokens stay and the slice-19 4th token is appended.
steps.mutation_note: The per-count `.ok()` degrade (failed countered-peer read → None → render_countered(None) → "(— countered)" on BOTH surfaces while the siblings — incl. the slice-18 own count — stay Some(_) → number, never a 5xx, never a fabricated 0, and the /peer-claims list rows + slice-13 flags still render) is load-bearing no-crash logic; mutation-test it so a mutant that fabricates 0 on failure, blanks the sibling counts / the slice-18 own line / the list rows, couples the peer fault to the own count, or propagates a 5xx is killed. The `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated 4th-distinct seam + the appended xtask VIEWER_FAIL_SEAM_TOKENS guard token are the release-safety enforcement (the seam never ships).

steps.name: The countered count never re-weights the peer-claims count (additive, own line untouched) and never re-orders / filters / re-weights the /peer-claims list (byte-identical baseline)
steps.description: The additive / no-regression edges (PC-NO-REWEIGHT, US-PC-001 Theme 5 / C-4 / WD-PC-7 + PC-NO-REORDER, US-PC-002 Theme 5 / C-4 / WD-PC-9). (PC-NO-REWEIGHT) Over a store with 4 peer claims (1 countered) AND the slice-18 own 12+3 countered, GET / renders the peer-claims count EXACTLY (the countered count is additive awareness, never a deduction — the number is NOT total-minus-countered), the slice-18 own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" is BYTE-UNTOUCHED (assert_landing_own_line_untouched, WD-PC-7), and the copy carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral). (PC-NO-REORDER) Over the slice-13 mixed-pages fixture (a mix of countered + un-countered peer claims spanning the page, with a known countered subset ≥ 1 pinned by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable / no vacuous pass), GET /peer-claims renders the additive header count AND the list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, every row's verbatim confidence + peer origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count baseline (the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped). REUSES the slice-13 byte-identity assert (assert_peer_claims_order_byte_identical, which elides the additive flag anchors; the header "(N countered)" lives OUTSIDE the row body). Confirmatory once 01-01/01-02 exist; pins that the additive header text re-ordered / filtered / re-weighted NOTHING (C-4 / WD-PC-9) and the slice-18 own surfaces are untouched (WD-PC-7).
criteria: GET / over a store with 4 peer claims (1 countered) AND the slice-18 own 12+3, renders the peer-claims count EXACTLY (additive, never a deduction — the body never shows "total-1 peer claims"); the front door carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).
The slice-18 own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" is BYTE-UNTOUCHED on the landing (assert_landing_own_line_untouched, WD-PC-7) — this slice adds JUST the peer count, no re-touch of the own count.

GET /peer-claims over the slice-13 mixed-pages fixture (known countered subset ≥ 1, pinned by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable) RENDERS the additive header count (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_count(expected)) — the feature is present, killing a vacuous pass.

The /peer-claims list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, every row's verbatim confidence + peer origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count slice-06/07 baseline (assert_peer_claims_order_byte_identical) — the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped (C-4 / WD-PC-9).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a store with 4 peer claims (1 countered) AND the slice-18 own 12+3, renders the peer-claims count EXACTLY (additive, never a deduction — the body never shows "total-1 peer claims"); the front door carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).
The slice-18 own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" is BYTE-UNTOUCHED on the landing (assert_landing_own_line_untouched, WD-PC-7) — this slice adds JUST the peer count, no re-touch of the own count.

GET /peer-claims over the slice-13 mixed-pages fixture (known countered subset ≥ 1, pinned by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable) RENDERS the additive header count (assert_peer_claims_header_countered_count(expected)) — the feature is present, killing a vacuous pass.

The /peer-claims list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, every row's verbatim confidence + peer origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count slice-06/07 baseline (assert_peer_claims_order_byte_identical) — the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped (C-4 / WD-PC-9).
steps.mutation_note: The additive-no-regression property (the peer-claims count is never deducted by the countered count; the slice-18 own line is byte-untouched; the /peer-claims list order/paging/total/confidence/origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline) is load-bearing; mutation-test render_landing + render_peer_claims_page so a mutant that deducts the countered count from the peer-claims number, re-touches the slice-18 own line, or re-orders / filters / groups the list by countered presence, is killed.

steps.name: Read-only, offline, no-N+1, anti-misread on both surfaces — the peer count is render-only LOCAL text, neutral, invariant to store size
steps.description: The remaining cross-cutting story edges that flip green for free once 01-01..02-02 exist (PC-READONLY-LANDING/HEADER, Theme 6 / C-1 CARDINAL; PC-OFFLINE-LANDING/HEADER, Theme 7 / C-2 CARDINAL; PC-NO-N-PLUS-1, Theme 8 / C-3 CARDINAL; PC-ANTI-MISREAD, Theme 9 / C-6 / WD-PC-10). (PC-READONLY-*) GET / and GET /peer-claims over a seeded counter-aware peer store render the countered count AND carry NO `<form>`/`<button>`/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the countered count is render-only text, never a sort/filter/mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control; C-1 CARDINAL, the slice-06 no-key guarantee inherited). (PC-OFFLINE-*) Both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (the count is a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate with NO outbound edge — offline-STRONGER than /search/scrape: no PDS fetch, no DID re-resolution, no peer pull) and reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2). (PC-NO-N-PLUS-1) A LARGE store (the seeded 1-countered peer store + 120 more PLAIN cached peer claims via seed_extra_plain_peer_claims, the countered count pinned STILL 1 by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle) renders the right count (1) in ONE request — the aggregate read returns the right total invariant to store size, no per-claim counter-presence loop (the strict 1-query bound is a DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit test). (PC-ANTI-MISREAD) Over the one-peer-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.40), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral). Confirmatory once the render + aggregate + REUSED helper exist; pins read-only / offline / no-N+1 / neutral-copy on the OBSERVABLE surfaces.
criteria: GET / and GET /peer-claims over a seeded counter-aware peer store render the countered count AND carry NO `<form>`/`<button>`/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the count is render-only text, never a sort/filter/mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control; C-1 CARDINAL).

Both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate, NO outbound edge) and reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js — references_external_cdn() is false (C-2 / KPI-HX-G2).

A LARGE store (1 countered peer claim + 120 more plain cached peer claims; the countered count pinned STILL 1 by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle) renders "(1 countered)" in ONE request — the aggregate read is invariant to store size, no per-claim loop (C-3 CARDINAL).

Over the one-peer-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.40), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral; C-6 / WD-PC-10).

A LARGE store (1 countered peer claim + 120 more plain cached peer claims; the countered count pinned STILL 1 by the read_countered_peer_claims_count oracle) renders "(1 countered)" in ONE request — the aggregate read is invariant to store size, no per-claim loop (C-3 CARDINAL).

Over the one-peer-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.40), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral; C-6 / WD-PC-10).
steps.mutation_note: The render-only-ness (no mutating control), offline (no outbound edge / vendored-only htmx), no-N+1 (one aggregate read invariant to store size), and neutral anti-misread copy are load-bearing on both surfaces; mutation-test render_landing/render_peer_claims_page/render_countered so a mutant that wraps the count in a control, adds a CDN reference, introduces a per-claim loop, or emits verdict/penalty/"disputed by N" copy is killed.

phases.name: Gold guardrail invariants (countered-peer count — read-only, no-write, offline-chrome, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, presence-once, single-source, no-regression, own-untouched)

steps.name: Gold: the ten countered-peer-count guardrail invariants (read-only, no-write, offline-chrome, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, presence-once, single-source, no-regression, slice-18-own-untouched)
steps.description: The ten cross-cutting GOLD guardrails (PC-INV-*) that must hold over the WHOLE countered-peer-count surface on `GET /` + `GET /peer-claims` — the BEHAVIORAL layer of the three-layer ADR-056 enforcement (type [StoreReadPort declares no mutation method; count_countered_peer_claims returns Result<usize, _>] + xtask [check_viewer_capability_boundary + no_cross_table_join_elides_author, GREEN by construction — R-PC-9, `(peer=T, own=F)` → None] are the other two). They drive the REAL openlore ui subprocess + HTTP over a REAL seeded LOCAL DuckDB, no mocked boundary (the count is a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read + PURE render). (PC-INV-ReadOnly, C-1 / WD-PC-1) exercising GET / + GET /peer-claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (universe-bound assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe; the countered count is computed per request and persists nothing). (PC-INV-NoWrite, C-1 CARDINAL) neither GET / nor GET /peer-claims renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the count is render-only text (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both). (PC-INV-OfflineChrome, C-2 / KPI-HX-G2) both pages reference ONLY the local /static/htmx.min.js, NO CDN (references_external_cdn() false). (PC-INV-Offline, C-2 / KPI-5) both surfaces render the countered-peer count fully network-down (a LOCAL read with NO outbound edge). (PC-INV-NoNPlus1, C-3 CARDINAL) a LARGE store (200 extra plain cached peer claims; the countered count pinned STILL 1 by the oracle) renders the right count in ONE request — the read count is invariant to store size. (PC-INV-MissingNotZero, C-2 / C-5 / WD-PC-6 CARDINAL) BOTH sides: a none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while a failed countered-peer read (via the 02-01 fault seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — a fabricated 0 on failure is unrepresentable (the shell maps a failed read to None, never Some(0)). (PC-INV-PresenceOnce, C-4 / BR-PC-1 CARDINAL) a peer claim countered by TWO distinct counterers contributes ONCE — BOTH the landing and the /peer-claims header render "(1 countered)", never "(2 countered)". (PC-INV-SingleSource, WD-PC-8) the landing "(N countered)" == the /peer-claims header "(N countered)" for the same store. (PC-INV-NoRegression, C-4 / WD-PC-9) the /peer-claims list order/paging/count/confidence/origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline. (PC-INV-OwnUntouched, BR-PC-4 / WD-PC-7) the slice-18 own-claims countered count (landing own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" + the /claims header "(3 countered)") still renders UNCHANGED beside the new peer count — this slice adds JUST the peer count, no third dimension, the slice-18 own surfaces byte-untouched (guards R-PC-8 scope-creep). These INHERIT the slice-06/07/17/18 viewer golds (viewer_is_read_only / store_views_work_offline / web_process_holds_no_signing_key / the slice-17 landing golds / the slice-18 own-count golds) and add the COUNTERED-PEER-COUNT specifics. Flip green for free once 01-01..02-03 exist (the MissingNotZero failure side reuses the 02-01 fault seam; the OwnUntouched gold reuses the slice-18 own surfaces shipped + untouched). LAST roadmap step.
criteria: PC-INV-ReadOnly + PC-INV-NoWrite: exercising GET / + GET /peer-claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe), and neither response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both) — the count is render-only text, the viewer holds no key (C-1 CARDINAL / WD-PC-1).

PC-INV-OfflineChrome + PC-INV-Offline: both pages reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND both surfaces render the countered-peer count fully network-down with NO outbound edge (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2 / KPI-5).

PC-INV-NoNPlus1 + PC-INV-MissingNotZero + PC-INV-PresenceOnce: a LARGE store (200 extra plain cached peer claims, countered count pinned STILL 1) renders the right count in ONE request invariant to store size; the none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and a peer claim countered by TWO counterers renders "(1 countered)" on BOTH surfaces, never "(2 countered)".

PC-INV-SingleSource + PC-INV-NoRegression + PC-INV-OwnUntouched: the landing "(N countered)" == the /peer-claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (assert_landing_and_peer_claims_countered_consistent, WD-PC-8); the /peer-claims list order/paging/count/confidence/origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline (assert_peer_claims_order_byte_identical, C-4 / WD-PC-9); and the slice-18 own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" + the /claims header "(3 countered)" render UNTOUCHED beside the new peer count (assert_landing_own_line_untouched + assert_claims_header_countered_count, BR-PC-4 / WD-PC-7).
acceptance_criteria: PC-INV-ReadOnly + PC-INV-NoWrite: exercising GET / + GET /peer-claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe), and neither response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both) — the count is render-only text, the viewer holds no key (C-1 CARDINAL / WD-PC-1).

PC-INV-OfflineChrome + PC-INV-Offline: both pages reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND both surfaces render the countered-peer count fully network-down with NO outbound edge (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2 / KPI-5).

PC-INV-NoNPlus1 + PC-INV-MissingNotZero + PC-INV-PresenceOnce: a LARGE store (200 extra plain cached peer claims, countered count pinned STILL 1) renders the right count in ONE request invariant to store size; the none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and a peer claim countered by TWO counterers renders "(1 countered)" on BOTH surfaces, never "(2 countered)".

PC-INV-SingleSource + PC-INV-NoRegression + PC-INV-OwnUntouched: the landing "(N countered)" == the /peer-claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (assert_landing_and_peer_claims_countered_consistent, WD-PC-8); the /peer-claims list order/paging/count/confidence/origin + the slice-13 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline (assert_peer_claims_order_byte_identical, C-4 / WD-PC-9); and the slice-18 own line "12 own claims (3 countered)" + the /claims header "(3 countered)" render UNTOUCHED beside the new peer count (assert_landing_own_line_untouched + assert_claims_header_countered_count, BR-PC-4 / WD-PC-7).
steps.mutation_note: These ten guardrails are the behavioral layer of the ADR-056 three-layer enforcement; ensure mutation testing of the pure render core (render_countered Some(n)→"(n countered)" / Some(0)→"(0 countered)" / None→"(— countered)", the additive peer-claims render, the slice-18 own-line untouched, no-control, no-CDN, neutral copy) + the COUNT(DISTINCT) presence aggregate over peer_claims + the per-count `.ok()` degrade is green so the gold guardrails are not the only line of defense. The presence-once (count(distinct)), single-source (landing==header), no-regression (byte-identical list), missing≠zero (both sides), no-N+1 (invariant to store size), and slice-18-own-untouched are the countered-peer-count-specific additions over the inherited slice-06/07/17/18 golds.

validation.note: APPROVED — all 7 dimensions PASS; 7 steps/26 scenarios
